title: West Seattle Viewpoints with Best Views of Seattle
content: Admiral Viewpoint (or Admiral Way Viewpoint — map) used to offer a fantastic view of Seattle but now you have to find a hole in the canopy to see much at all.

title: Belvedere Park - Parks | seattle.gov
content: # Belvedere Park. 3600 SW Admiral Wy., Seattle, WA 98126. Phone: (206) 684-4075 684-4075). ## Amenities. Views Art in the Park. This park has a stunning view across Elliott Bay to downtown Seattle, and on clear days to the Cascade Mountains and Mt. Rainier. Divided into two parts on the east and west sides of Admiral Way, the east section features a totem pole depicting stylized beavers, fish and frogs. Belvedere Viewpoint is a popular spot for scenic photos and as a backdrop for wedding and other ceremonial photos. ## Parks and Recreation. Mailing Address: 100 Dexter Ave N, Seattle, WA, 98109. Fax: (206) 615-1813 615-1813). Email: pks\_info@seattle.gov. Sign Up for Latest Updates. ## Citywide Information. ### Popular Pages. 4. Find a Restroom in a Park. Healthy People, Thriving Environment, Vibrant Community.

title: HAMILTON VIEWPOINT PARK - Updated June 2026 - Yelp
content: Great place to get a view of the Seattle skyline at night with the reflection off the water. Easy parking, really dark, but felt pretty safe as a solo tourist.

title: Best Seattle Viewpoints you don’t want to miss! The Ultimate City Guide - Miss Rover
content: ## Best Seattle Viewpoints you don’t want to miss! I’ve been meaning to make a guide for Seattle for WAY too long. I lived there for 3 years and it was one of the best times of my life. This has allowed me to experience Seattle as both a resident and as a tourist. Kerry Park is one of my favorite viewpoints in Seattle. You get a fantastic view of downtown Seattle, the Space Needle, and if you’re lucky – Mount Rainier. I walked here when staying in Queen Anne and there’s a series of steps to get up to the top viewpoint that definitely gave me a workout! **Crowd Rating:** On the weekends this place can get pretty crowded, especially if the weather is nice and the mountain is out. It is right at the edge of a neighborhood and you would have to get pretty lucky to snag a spot nearby.
